meaning: n.切努克人[语](北美印第安之一族)
examples: Which means the Chinook makes for a bigger target.
I caught a flight from Da Nang on a Chinook and was there in no time.
In February 2007, a Chinook helicopter crashed in Zabul, killing eight U.S. personnel.
They have large flight decks to accommodate helicopters up to the size of a Chinook.
The group is upgrading Chinook helicopters and training soldiers as well as testing weapons.
